hub_subtitle: Business English para as reuniões com o time Global da Sanofi, congressos médicos e a apresentação de agosto

lesson.grammar_point: going to for future plans already decided (consolidation)
lesson.menu_title: Plans in Motion -- Talking About What's Coming Next
lesson.menu_desc: Reading model: the Rain Plan for Q3 -- how to present a plan already decided (going to), the kickoff, the milestones, the rollout and the forecast for the Global team -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 12 -- Plans in Motion (Going to Consolidation)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Emmanuele Orrico | Lesson 12 -- Plans in Motion (Going to Consolidation)

listenings.text: Emmanuele, Marc Dubois here. Before your presentation to Paris next week, I want to know the plan for the next quarter. Keep it simple. First: what is the team going to launch, and when? Second: how is the rollout going to happen -- all at once, or in phases? And third: what is the forecast for new patients? Do not send me a long report. Tell me the story of the plan in a few clear sentences. And one more thing: I want to know what you are going to do if a target is not met. Talk to you next week.

Good morning, everyone. Claire Bennett, with the global plan for the next quarter. Here is what is coming. In July, we are going to kick off three new initiatives across the region. In August, the biggest milestone: we are going to launch the higher dose in several countries. The rollout is going to happen in phases, not all at once, and the timeline is already on the shared drive. The budget is approved, and the forecast for new patients is strong. Before your local presentations, please send me two things: the kickoff date for your country, and your forecast for the quarter. Thank you, everyone.

extra_audio.text: Good morning, Marc. Here is the plan for Brazil for the third quarter. In July, we are going to start the kickoff of the new initiative. In August, we are going to launch the higher dose -- that is the biggest milestone. The rollout is going to happen in three phases, and the forecast for new patients is strong.

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a new plan or project to reach a goal
a statement about future numbers, based on today's data
the official start of a project or a plan
one stage of a bigger plan
the money a team plans to spend on a project
a plan that shows when each step is going to happen
to make something bigger, or grow into new areas
a simple plan of the steps to reach a goal

paras: Every year, the immunology team at Sanofi Brazil writes a business plan for the next months. This year the plan has a name: the Rain Plan. It is a simple roadmap for the third quarter, and it answers one question for the Global team in Paris: what is Brazil going to do next? The plan is not a dream. Every line is a decision that is already made.

So here is the plan. In July, the team is going to start the kickoff of the new atopic dermatitis initiative. In August, they are going to launch the higher dose in the private sector -- that is the biggest milestone of the quarter. The rollout is going to happen in three phases, region by region, and the timeline is on the wall for everyone to see. The team is also going to expand the program to two new regions. The budget is ready, and the forecast for new patients is strong.

Emmanuele is going to present all of this to Paris in ninety days. She is not going to read a long report; she is going to tell the story of the plan in a few clear sentences. When Global asks 'what is going to happen if a target is not met?', she is going to have an answer. A good manager does not only report the past -- she says, with confidence, what the team is going to do next.

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: The Rain Plan is only a dream, and nobody knows if it is going to happen.
The Rain Plan is a clear plan of decisions already made for Q3 -- and Emmanuele is going to present what the team is going to do next, using going to.
Emmanuele is going to read a very long report to the Global team in Paris.

items: The Rain Plan is a roadmap for the third quarter.
The first paragraph says exactly this: "It is a simple roadmap for the third quarter."
The lines of the plan are only ideas that are not decided yet.
The opposite: "The plan is not a dream. Every line is a decision that is already made."
The rollout of the higher dose is going to happen all at once.
The text says the rollout "is going to happen in three phases, region by region", not all at once.
Emmanuele is going to tell the story of the plan in a few clear sentences, not a long report.
The last paragraph: "She is not going to read a long report; she is going to tell the story of the plan in a few clear sentences."

parts: Good morning, Marc. Here is the plan for the quarter. In July, the team
start the kickoff of the new initiative. In August, we
launch the higher dose -- that is the biggest milestone. The rollout
expand the program to two new regions, and the forecast for new patients
present all of this to Paris in ninety days.

gapfill.text: One pattern does all the work here: SUBJECT + am / is / are + GOING TO + base verb. The verb after "going to" never changes: going to launch, going to happen, going to expand -- never "going to launches", never "going to launched". Use it for a plan that is already decided: the kickoff, the rollout, the forecast. It is the language of a business plan.

bank.label: Useful language for presenting a plan
items: Here is the plan for the next quarter.
In July, we are going to start the kickoff of the new initiative.
In August, we are going to launch the higher dose.
The rollout is going to happen in three phases.
The forecast for new patients is strong.
The plan is to expand the program to two new regions.
I am going to present all of this to Paris in ninety days.

lf.title: One plan, one pattern: going to + base verb
items: expand the program to two new regions.
present the plan to Paris in ninety days.

lf.text: Notice the base verb after "going to": start, happen, expand, present. It never changes, in any of the four lines. The only thing that changes is am / is / are, and that follows the subject: I am, the rollout is, we are. When the plan is already decided, this is the pattern Global wants to hear.

items.situation: Marc opens the call: "So, what is the plan for the quarter?" Give him the headline in one sentence.
tips: Here is the plan for Q3. In July, we are going to start the kickoff of the new initiative.
The plan is ready. In August, we are going to launch the higher dose -- the biggest milestone.

items.situation: "When are you going to launch the higher dose?"
tips: We are going to launch it in August, in the private sector.
The launch is in August -- that is the biggest milestone of the quarter.

items.situation: "How is the rollout going to happen -- all at once?"
tips: No, the rollout is going to happen in three phases, region by region.
In phases. We are going to start with two regions, then expand.

items.situation: "What is the forecast for new patients?"
tips: The forecast is strong, and the budget is already approved.
We are going to reach more new patients this quarter -- the forecast is strong.

items.situation: "What are you going to do if a target is not met?"
tips: If a target is not met, we are going to adjust the timeline and focus on two key regions.
We are going to review the forecast every month, so we can act early.

items.situation: You want to know if Paris has news on the budget before the call ends. Ask Marc.
tips: Are there any updates on the budget from your side?
Is Global going to confirm the budget before the kickoff?
